# Contributing to dijidaro.
We welcome contributions from anyone who is interested in improving dijidaro. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just getting started, there are many ways that you can help us make our web app better.

## How to contribute
1. Fork the repository on GitHub.
2. Clone your forked repository to your local machine.
3. Create a new branch for your changes.
4. Make your changes and commit them to your branch.
5. Push your changes to your forked repository on GitHub.
6. Open a pull request from your branch to the main repository.
7. Wait for a maintainer to review your pull request.

## Code of conduct
We expect all contributors to adhere to our [Code of Conduct](.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) when participating in our project.

## Reporting bugs
If you find a bug in our web app, please report it by opening an issue on GitHub. Please include a detailed description of the bug, including steps to reproduce it.

## Feature requests
If you have an idea for a new feature or improvement to our web app, please let us know by opening an issue on GitHub. Please include a detailed description of the feature or improvement, including why you think it would be valuable.

## Pull request guidelines
When submitting a pull request, please:
- Provide a clear and detailed description of the changes you have made.
- Include any necessary documentation updates.
- Include tests for your changes, if applicable.
- Ensure that your code follows our [style guide](.github/STYLE_GUIDE.md).

## Code reviews
All pull requests will be reviewed by a maintainer. The maintainer may request changes or ask for clarifications. Please respond to all review comments in a timely manner.

Thank you
We appreciate all contributions to dijidaro. Thank you for helping us make our web app better!
